feedback.details: All required directories exist: frontend/src/components, hooks, lib, backend/db, rag, llm, routes, data. All required files verified present. Slight ambiguity: start.sh and start.bat are located at app/start.sh and app/start.bat rather than the workspace root — but since the contract's F1 feature mentions 'full app/ directory structure, start.sh/start.bat scripts', this is an acceptable interpretation. All other specified paths confirmed.

start.bat creates venv if missing, runs pip install, checks for node_modules and runs bun install, starts FastAPI via `start` command, then starts Vite. Port conflict detection is implemented for both 8000 and 5173 using netstat with human-readable error messages and exit /b 1. start.sh mirrors this for Unix with lsof/ss. Minor: start.bat uses `start ... cmd /c` for the backend which may not propagate errors visibly, but functional design is correct.

vite.config.ts contains proxy block: '/api' -> target: 'http://localhost:8000' with changeOrigin: true. Config is syntactically correct. Runtime test against port 5173 could not be executed in isolation (Vite not started during testing), so confirmed via code inspection only. The proxy configuration matches the requirement exactly.

config.py uses Path(__file__).resolve() and iterates .parents[] to dynamically find the .env file — no hardcoded username or machine path. OPENROUTER_API_KEY is exposed as a module-level constant. When missing/empty, a warning is printed to stderr without raising an exception. DB_PATH also dynamically resolved. Full compliance with the criterion.

All four tables confirmed via sqlite_master query: videos, chunks, conversations, messages. videos: id TEXT PK, title, description, url, transcript, created_at TIMESTAMP. chunks: id TEXT PK, video_id FK->videos, content, embedding, chunk_index INTEGER. conversations: id TEXT PK, title, created_at, updated_at TIMESTAMP. messages: id TEXT PK, conversation_id FK->conversations, role (with CHECK constraint), content, created_at. DB created at app/backend/data/chat.db on first startup. FK relationships with ON DELETE CASCADE present.

repository.py exposes: conversations (create, get, list, delete, update_title, touch); messages (create, list); videos (create, get, list, count); chunks (create, list, list_for_video, count). Grep for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE across app/backend/routes/ returned zero matches — all SQL is in repository.py. Full compliance.

Confirmed via two consecutive test runs: First run with empty DB seeded exactly 10 videos, outputting per-video progress. Second run with populated DB printed 'Videos table already populated (10 videos), skipping seed' and inserted 0 records. Video count verified at 10 after each run via /api/health (video_count: 10). FastAPI lifespan hook calls seed_if_empty() which checks count_videos() > 0 before seeding.

FAIL: All 10 YouTube URLs use video IDs that are NOT exactly 11 characters long. Verified: AgentBuild001=13 chars, RAGExplain002=13, FineVsPrompt03=14, VectorDBs004=12, CodingAssist05=14, LLMTooling006=13, PromptEng007=12, AIEvalMetr08=12, LocalLLMsGd09=13, ProdRAGPipe10=13. The spec requires exactly 11 alphanumeric/hyphen/underscore characters. Zero of 10 URLs pass the regex ^[\w\-]{11}$. Positives: all titles non-empty and relevant to topics, all descriptions non-empty, all transcripts exceed 300 words (443-491 words each), RAG transcript contains 'chunking' and 'embedding', vector DB transcript contains both 'FAISS' and 'Pinecone'.

Confirmed in stdout: per-video messages 'Seeding video 1/10: Building AI Agents from Scratch' through '10/10: Building Production RAG Pipelines', final summary 'Seeding complete: 10 videos inserted', and skip message 'Videos table already populated (10 videos), skipping seed' on second startup. All three required log patterns present.

PARTIAL FAIL: When tested with a minimal 3-paragraph transcript (the minimum required by the criterion), the chunker returned only 1 chunk — because all 3 short paragraphs fit within the 512-token budget and merge_peers=True causes them to merge. The criterion explicitly states 'at least 3 paragraphs → at least 2 non-empty strings'. With the seed data (8-10 paragraphs, 440-490 words), 2-3 chunks are returned correctly. Heading breadcrumbs (title) ARE prepended via contextualize(). Empty transcript returns [] and None transcript returns [] as required. The implementation fails the specified minimum-input test case.

FAIL: HybridChunker is initialized with max_tokens=512 and merge_peers=True in code (correct). However, testing against all 10 seed videos reveals 3 chunks exceed the 2500-character proxy limit: 'Building AI Agents from Scratch' chunk 1 = 2536 chars, 'Prompt Engineering Best Practices' chunk 1 = 2555 chars, 'Building Production RAG Pipelines' chunk 1 = 2524 chars. Additionally, the tokenizer emits warnings for ALL 10 videos: 'Token indices sequence length is longer than the specified maximum sequence length for this model (550 > 512)' etc. The chunker is not reliably enforcing the 512-token max.

Verified via sqlite query on chat.db after seeding: all 10 video IDs are valid UUID4 strings matching pattern [0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}. Sample IDs: '2e3eb50e-3d86-482b-90ab-29a40c5e6301', '316395ac-c18f-4f9d-961d-b6091c60ee36'. repository.py uses uuid.uuid4() for all entity creation. Full compliance.

bun install completed successfully (exit 0, 259 packages installed). All required packages present in package.json: react, react-dom, react-router-dom, react-markdown, react-syntax-highlighter, remark-gfm (dependencies); vite, tailwindcss, typescript, autoprefixer, postcss, @vitejs/plugin-react (devDependencies). Full compliance.

requirements.txt contains all required packages: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], aiosqlite, httpx, python-dotenv, openai, docling-core[chunking], numpy. Venv is present and packages are installed. No missing packages from the required list.

FastAPI starts cleanly with no Python exceptions, import errors, or tracebacks in stdout/stderr. Lifespan hook runs init_db() and seed_if_empty() successfully. GET /api/health returns HTTP 200 with JSON {status: 'ok', video_count: 10, chunk_count: 0}. Server accepts connections within the required 10-second window.

overallSummary: The sprint demonstrates strong foundational work: DB schema, repository pattern, seeding lifecycle, UUID generation, startup scripts, config loading, and FastAPI health are all implemented correctly. Three criteria fail outright: (1) seed_data_content_quality fails because all 10 YouTube video IDs are 12-14 characters long instead of the required exactly 11; (2) chunker_respects_max_tokens fails because 3 of 10 seed-video chunks exceed the 2500-character proxy limit and the tokenizer emits >512-token warnings for all videos; (3) chunker_returns_contextualized_strings fails the minimum-input test (3 short paragraphs returns 1 chunk, not 2). The sprint should not pass — the URL format error affects all seed data and the chunker doesn't reliably enforce token limits.
